feeds.title: bioRxiv Subject Collection: Bioinformatics
feeds.tldr: This bioinformatics digest presents tools for CRISPR screen analysis, benchmarks for long-read RNA-seq, a platform for cancer mutation visualization, a pipeline for methylation phasing, proteomics of ER stress, improved DIA proteomics with variability, and a method for tumor data anonymization. These works provide resources for functional genomics, technology selection, interactive analysis, epigenetic profiling, protein function studies, bacterial proteotyping, and privacy-preserving data sharing in oncology.
highlights: Correlate web app enables data-driven analysis of gene dependencies from CRISPR screens.
Benchmarking offers guidance on long-read RNA-seq technologies and DIA proteomics methods.
OncoMORPHIA and PANDA advance cancer mutation visualization and single-molecule methylation analysis.

items.title: Correlate: A Web Application for Analyzing Gene Sets and Exploring Gene Dependencies Using CRISPR Screen Data
items.tldr: Correlate is a web application that uses Cancer Dependency Map CRISPR screen data to identify functionally linked genes and explore dependencies, providing a data-driven alternative to knowledge-based tools like STRING.

items.title: Benchmarking long-read RNA-seq across modalities, methods, and sequencing depth in iNeurons
items.tldr: A benchmark study compares long-read RNA-seq technologies and quantification methods in neurons, revealing platform-specific biases (e.g., PB under-detects short transcripts) and recommending tools like Isosceles, with single-cell requiring three- to four-fold greater depth than bulk.

items.title: OncoMORPHIA: An Integrated Web Platform for Interactive 3D Visualization and Functional Annotation of Cancer Mutations
items.tldr: OncoMORPHIA is a free web platform that integrates 3D protein structure visualization with clinical annotation for cancer mutations, automatically retrieving data from ten public databases for interactive exploration of 45 major driver genes.

items.title: PANDA: Read-Level Phased Analysis of DNA Amplicons for Methylation Studies
items.tldr: PANDA is a graphical pipeline that phases bisulfite sequencing reads to reconstruct contiguous single-molecule methylation patterns, enabling analysis of epiallelic heterogeneity in targeted amplicon studies and supporting both Sanger and NGS inputs.

items.title: Proteome analyses reveal Endoplasmic Reticulum stress-induced changes in protein abundance associated with Ube2j2 deficiency in human cell culture
items.tldr: Proteomics analysis of Ube2j2-deficient cells under ER stress identifies twelve protein abundance modules, revealing known UPR/ERAD pathways and new associations with RNA metabolism and cell-cycle progression.

items.title: Importance of taking Single Amino Acid Variant and accessory proteome variability into account in Data Independent Acquisition Proteomics: illustrated with Legionella pneumophila analysis
items.tldr: A workflow incorporating protein sequence variability in DIA proteomics improves coverage and identification confidence for bacterial isolates, capturing 28-77% of variant-specific sequences in Legionella pneumophila while maintaining low false positives.

items.title: Anonymized Somatic Tumor Twins (STTs) enable open genome data sharing and use in research and clinical oncology
items.tldr: GenomeAnonymizer generates anonymized Somatic Tumor Twins from tumor-normal pair data, preserving somatic variants and sequencing noise to enable privacy-protected open sharing for research and clinical oncology.

items.title: LigandForge: A Web Server for Structure-Guided De Novo Drug Design
items.tldr: Despite advances in computational drug discovery, de novo drug design remains hindered by high licensing costs and the need for specialized programming expertise. We present LigandForge, a webserver for structure-guided de novo ligand generation.

items.title: Conserved water molecules as structural ligands modulating pathogenic variation in human protein binding sites
items.tldr: Conserved water molecules (CWMs) are tightly bound solvent molecules that occupy well-defined and recurrent positions in protein structures. Although they are known to influence protein stability, function, and ligand binding, their contribution to human gene…

items.title: muat: portable transformer-based method for tumour classification and representation learning from somatic variants
items.tldr: Motivation: Deep neural networks have proven effective in classifying tumour types using next-generation sequencing data. However, developing transferable models that work across heterogeneous operating environments remains challenging due to differences in c…

items.title: EMITS: expectation-maximization abundance estimation for fungal ITS communities from long-read sequencing
items.tldr: EMITS: expectation-maximization abundance estimation for fungal ITS communities from long-read sequencing

items.title: Towards a Cytometry Foundation Model: Interpretable Sample-level Predictive Modelling via Pretrained Transformers
items.tldr: Towards a Cytometry Foundation Model: Interpretable Sample-level Predictive Modelling via Pretrained Transformers

items.title: TF-IDF k-mer-based Classical and Hybrid Machine Learning Models for SARS-CoV-2 Variant Classification under Imbalanced Genomic Data
items.tldr: TF-IDF k-mer-based Classical and Hybrid Machine Learning Models for SARS-CoV-2 Variant Classification under Imbalanced Genomic Data

items.title: Benchmarking Heritability Estimation Strategies Across 86 Configurations and Their Downstream Effect on Polygenic Risk Score Performance
items.tldr: Benchmarking Heritability Estimation Strategies Across 86 Configurations and Their Downstream Effect on Polygenic Risk Score Performance

feeds.title: Cell
feeds.tldr: This digest features a Cell paper on cellular senescence in cancer. It defines six major hallmarks of senescence and describes how this program halts cell division while rewiring cellular processes to either suppress or promote cancer, highlighting its potential for precision therapies.
highlights: Cellular senescence is defined by six major hallmarks.
Senescence halts cell division and rewires chromatin, metabolism, and immune interactions.
It has dual roles in cancer suppression and promotion, offering therapeutic promise.

items.title: Senescence in cancer: Hallmarks, paradoxes, and therapeutic promise
items.tldr: The paper reviews cellular senescence in cancer, detailing its six hallmarks and paradoxical effects on tumor growth, and emphasizes its emerging role in precision cancer therapy.

feeds.title: Nature
feeds.tldr: The Trump administration has proposed massive budget cuts for US science agencies in the 2027 fiscal year, including over 50% reductions for NSF and EPA, elimination of NSF's social sciences division, and a ban on federal journal funding. While increasing military spending by 44% and maintaining some quantum and AI research, the plan faces congressional negotiation where similar cuts were rejected in 2026.
highlights: NSF budget cut 55% to $4B; social sciences directorate eliminated
EPA and NASA science face >50% and 47% cuts; journal subscription ban imposed
Military funding up 44%; Congress to finalize budget after 2026 rejections

items.title: Massive budget cuts for US science proposed again by Trump administration
items.tldr: The Trump administration's 2027 budget proposal targets US science agencies with deep cuts, including a 55% reduction for NSF and elimination of its social sciences division, while maintaining funding for quantum and AI research but cutting basic NSF programs. Congress must approve the budget, having previously rejected similar reductions.

feeds.title: Nature Communications
feeds.tldr: This Nature Communications digest covers diverse research from materials science to environmental studies. Key advances include layer-selective control in graphene for logic gates, insights into microbial chemotaxis and phage dynamics, and a new approach to overcome cancer drug resistance. Neural mechanisms of social behavior and topological photonics are also featured, along with innovations in DNA storage, health data harmonization, and battery technology. Studies on glacial floods, lung development, permafrost thaw, and protein design for origins of life round out the collection.
highlights: Hydrogenation enables configurable logic gates in twisted bilayer graphene.
Nutrient repulsion improves microbial chemotaxis to moving sources.
UNI418 compound overcomes PARP inhibitor resistance by degrading HR proteins.

items.title: Layer-selective hydrogenation and proton transport in twisted bilayer graphene
items.tldr: Hydrogenation induces layer-selective conductor-insulator transitions in twisted bilayer graphene, creating an electrochemical interface for configurable logic gates.

items.title: Repulsion from slow-diffusing nutrients improves microbial chemotaxis towards moving sources
items.tldr: Adding repulsion from some nutrients to attraction to others improves microbial chemotaxis towards moving sources, as demonstrated with E. coli.

items.title: Targeting IP 6 signaling to destabilize homologous recombination proteins to overcome PARP inhibitor resistance
items.tldr: The compound UNI418 inhibits lipid kinases to reduce IP6, triggering degradation of homologous recombination proteins and overcoming PARP inhibitor resistance.

items.title: Functional synaptic connectivity shapes spine stability in the hippocampus
items.tldr: Synaptic strength predicts spine size and long-term stability in the hippocampus, with dendritic branches maintaining stable input despite synaptic variability.

items.title: Antibiotic resistance gene analyses in microbial communities: challenges and opportunities
items.tldr: This Comment discusses the lack of unified methods for identifying and interpreting antibiotic resistance genes in metagenomics data, outlining challenges and solutions.

items.title: Common and distinct neural correlates of social interaction processing and theory of mind in narratives
items.tldr: Recognizing social interactions in narratives engages theory of mind brain areas, while explicit theory of mind use recruits additional regions for action understanding.

items.title: Artificial gauge fields for sculpting topological modes on photonic chips
items.tldr: Combining artificial gauge fields with neural networks allows topological photonic modes to be transformed into arbitrary shapes on chips.

items.title: Gungnir codec enabling high error-tolerance and low-redundancy DNA storage through substantial computing power
items.tldr: Gungnir is a DNA storage codec that recovers data from single error-prone sequences, reducing redundancy and cost while improving reliability.

items.title: Complex temporal dynamics of phage-bacteria populations in an animal-associated marine system
items.tldr: Study of marine phage-bacteria dynamics identifies oysters as hotspots for gene exchange, explaining viral genome stability.

items.title: Disentangled autoencoding equivariant diffusion model for controlled generation of 3D molecules
items.tldr: A semantics-guided diffusion model enables precise and data-efficient multi-property control in 3D molecule generation for drug discovery.

items.title: Subglacial water flow and ice dynamics during glacial lake outburst floods observed from space
items.tldr: Satellite data tracks subglacial flood water draining ~1 km3 over 3 weeks along a 50 km path, providing insights into glacier dynamics.

items.title: Social reward outcompetes drug seeking dopaminergic ensembles to prevent relapse
items.tldr: Social reward and drug relapse activate distinct dopaminergic ensembles in the VTA that compete to regulate relapse behavior.

items.title: Representation learning to advance multi-institutional studies with electronic health record data from US and France
items.tldr: A framework integrating medical knowledge, LLMs, and graph learning harmonizes EHR data across institutions without sharing patient-level data.

items.title: Hippo signaling differentially regulates distal progenitor subpopulations and their transitional states to construct the mammalian lungs
items.tldr: Distal SOX9+ lung progenitors, regulated by Hippo signaling, control lung size through bifurcation and transition to form airway and alveolar cells.

items.title: Permafrost landsystems define regional variability in climate change effects on northern environments
items.tldr: Permafrost landsystems, defined by landform-ecosystem interactions and geological-climatic legacies, predict regional thaw consequences.

items.title: Mechanofusion-derived cathode composite microstructures with scalable mixed conducting matrix coatings for solid state batteries
items.tldr: Solvent-free mechanofusion applies mixed conducting matrix coatings on cathode particles, with quality tied to mixing stresses for scalable battery manufacturing.

items.title: A highly limited amino acid library from asteroid Bennu yields wide-ranging protein folds
items.tldr: AI-based protein design from a limited primordial amino acid library, informed by asteroid data, reveals a wide range of possible protein folds.

items.title: Probing ultrafast heating and ionization dynamics in solid density plasmas with time-resolved resonant X-ray absorption and emission
items.tldr: Combining resonant X-ray emission spectroscopy and absorption imaging from an XFEL probe achieves sub-picosecond resolution of heating and ionization in solid-density plasmas.

items.title: Solution-processed photovoltaic and thermoelectric hybrid systems with efficiency exceeding 50%
items.tldr: Solution-processed photovoltaic-thermoelectric hybrid systems optimized for heat utilization and current matching demonstrate a maximum efficiency of 50.28%.

items.title: A conversational multi-agent AI system for automated plant phenotyping
items.tldr: An AI system integrating large language models with a phenotype toolkit automates crop analysis, reducing technical barriers and enhancing accessibility.

items.title: Distinct mechanisms of replication stress induced by oncogenic RAS and cyclin E1 converge on R-loop-dependent fork reversal
items.tldr: Oncogenic RAS induces replication stress through ROS-driven replisome alterations, leading to R-loop-dependent fork reversal, with distinct mechanisms converging from RAS and cyclin E1.

items.title: Influence of the broadly neutralizing antibody VRC01 on HIV breakthrough virus populations in antibody-mediated prevention trials
items.tldr: Deep sequencing of AMP trial participants shows 38% multilineage HIV infections, including coinfection with sensitive and resistant viruses, and demonstrates VRC01 does not alter the transmission bottleneck.

feeds.title: Nature Computational Science
feeds.tldr: Nature Computational Science currently has 2 retained item(s) on the page. Key items include "Universal restoration of medical images" and "Benchmarking alignment methods for spatial transcriptomics data".
highlights: Universal restoration of medical images
Benchmarking alignment methods for spatial transcriptomics data

items.title: Universal restoration of medical images
items.tldr: Nature Computational Science, Published online: 03 April 2026; doi:10.1038/s43588-026-00975-1 A self-supervised foundation model, HorusEye, learns realistic noise directly from X-ray scans and enables robust tomography restoration across diverse modalities, s…

items.title: Benchmarking alignment methods for spatial transcriptomics data
items.tldr: Nature Computational Science, Published online: 03 April 2026; doi:10.1038/s43588-026-00977-z A benchmark to evaluate spatial alignment methods for spatial transcriptomics shows that the best approach is dataset dependent and offers actionable guidance for th…

feeds.title: Nature Health
feeds.tldr: This feed contains one paper on GLP-1 receptor drugs for longevity therapeutics. The paper's title advocates for rigorous testing of GLP-1 receptor agonists, while the body text classifies GLP-1 receptor antagonists as transitional longevity therapeutics requiring more evidence on their direct and indirect effects. The source material is sparse and repetitive.
highlights: GLP-1 receptor agonists should be rigorously tested as longevity therapeutics
GLP-1 receptor antagonists can be classified as transitional longevity therapeutics
More evidence is needed to assess direct and indirect effects

items.title: GLP-1 receptor agonists should be rigorously tested as longevity therapeutics
items.tldr: The paper's title calls for rigorous testing of GLP-1 receptor agonists as longevity therapeutics, but the provided text states that GLP-1 receptor antagonists are transitional longevity therapeutics needing more evidence on their direct and indirect effects.

feeds.title: Nature Medicine
feeds.tldr: Nature Medicine publishes studies on monkeypox virus surveillance in the Republic of the Congo, exposome impacts on brain aging across 34 countries, and target product profiles for Alzheimer's disease therapies. Findings highlight co-circulation of multiple monkeypox clades, differential aging effects from social and physical exposures, and benchmarks to accelerate therapeutic development.
highlights: Monkeypox clade IIb lineage A.2.2 detected with co-circulation of clades Ia, Ib, and IIb, emphasizing surveillance needs.
Social exposures linked to faster functional brain aging; physical exposures to faster structural brain aging.
Target product profiles outlined to delay or prevent symptomatic Alzheimer's disease and guide development.

items.title: Evidence of monkeypox virus clade IIb lineage A.2.2 in the Republic of the Congo and co-circulation of clade Ia, Ib and clade IIb
items.tldr: Whole-genome phylogenetic analysis identified monkeypox virus clade IIb lineage A.2.2 in the Republic of the Congo and co-circulation of clades Ia, Ib, and IIb, underscoring the importance of improved surveillance to mitigate recombination risks.

items.title: The exposome of brain aging across 34 countries
items.tldr: Exposome analyses across 34 countries showed that social exposures are associated with faster functional brain aging, while physical exposures are associated with faster structural brain aging.

items.title: Target product profiles for treatments to delay or prevent symptomatic Alzheimer’s disease
items.tldr: The authors propose target product profiles for therapies designed to delay or prevent the onset of clinical symptoms of Alzheimer's disease, aiming to accelerate development and provide clear benchmarks.

feeds.title: Nature Methods
feeds.tldr: Nature Methods currently has 1 retained item(s) on the page. Key items include "A toolkit for modeling cis -regulatory logic of enhancers at large scale".
highlights: A toolkit for modeling cis -regulatory logic of enhancers at large scale

items.title: A toolkit for modeling cis -regulatory logic of enhancers at large scale
items.tldr: Nature Methods, Published online: 04 April 2026; doi:10.1038/s41592-026-03058-1 Deciphering the genomic regulatory code driving cell type-specific gene regulation has been a research quest for decades. We present CREsted, a software package that provides data…

feeds.title: Nature Microbiology
feeds.tldr: This daily digest from Nature Microbiology presents three studies: one quantifying global microbial diversity through metagenomic analysis, another identifying a donor-controlled mechanism that inhibits plasmid transfer via nanotubes, and a third revealing how cholesterol accumulation in tuberculosis granulomas disrupts immune cell interactions and infection control.
highlights: Metagenomic analysis predicts hundreds of thousands of new bacterial and archaeal species, primarily hidden in unbinned contigs, with soils and aquatic environments as diversity hotspots.
YokF endonuclease blocks nanotube-mediated plasmid exchange in Bacillus subtilis, with functional homologues widespread in Gram-positive bacteria.
Infection-driven cholesterol overload in tuberculosis granulomas impairs macrophage–CD4+ T cell interactions and anti-tuberculosis immunity.

items.title: Unbinned contigs expand known diversity in the global microbiome
items.tldr: A comprehensive analysis of 92,187 metagenomes reveals that the vast majority of discoverable bacterial and archaeal species are hidden among unbinned contigs, predicting ~705,000 bacterial and ~27,000 archaeal species-level clades and identifying soils and aquatic environments as hotspots of undiscovered lineages.

items.title: A family of endonucleases blocks nanotube-mediated plasmid exchange
items.tldr: The YokF endonuclease in Bacillus subtilis provides a donor-controlled mechanism to limit plasmid transfer to recipient bacteria via nanotubes, with YokF functional homologues found across Gram-positive bacterial species.

items.title: Lipid accumulation in tuberculosis granulomas inhibits macrophage–CD4 + T cell interactions and infection control
items.tldr: Spatial transcriptomics and immunofluorescence microscopy of human and mouse samples show that infection-driven cholesterol overload in tuberculosis granulomas impairs macrophage–CD4+ T cell interactions and anti-tuberculosis immunity.
